title: ascolepis dipsacoides subsp. siamensis
intro: Ascolepis dipsacoides subsp. siamensis, also known as Thai Balm, is a plant species that belongs to the Apiaceae family. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ascolepis dipsacoides subsp. siamensis include pain relief, wound healing, and the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea. Herbal preparations of Thai Balm often involve infusions, decoctions, or topical applications of the plant's essential oils, which can be found in various forms, including creams, ointments, and essential oil extracts.
